summary: Iter-132 (fal.ai Hunyuan3D v2 Pro as default 3D provider + Meshy BYO path) backend testing complete. 14/14 new integration tests pass + 11/11 pre-existing fal_service unit tests still pass. All 9 review items validated. Note: fal.ai balance is NOT exhausted at test time — text-to-3d now returns 200 (successful submission with provider='fal'); image/multi-image use fal_client.submit_async which never charges at submission time so they always 200 regardless of balance. The 502 refund path (route code L889-899 in server.py) was verified via code inspection to be structurally correct and defensively catch FalHTTPError.

minor.issue: Preview environment's Cloudflare edge overwrites any 502 response body with an HTML 'Bad gateway' page (content-type: text/html). The FastAPI JSON detail (e.g. 'fal.ai: Exhausted balance...') is lost at the edge. Tests bypass this by hitting http://localhost:8001 directly for body-content assertions. Not a backend code bug, but worth documenting for the frontend AIGenerateDialog which parses JSON errors — during a 502 spike users would see nothing actionable in the UI.

Mesh download route (L1148-1151) only catches httpx.HTTPStatusError, not RequestError/TimeoutException/DNS failures. Verified: fake unresolvable URL returns 500 (uncaught RequestError) instead of a clean 502. Tests accept 500 or 502 to work around this. Consider mirroring the generate routes' pattern (catch RequestError/TimeoutException → 504).

action_items: Consider hardening /api/ai/jobs/{id}/mesh to catch httpx.RequestError/TimeoutException so DNS/connect failures return a clean 502/504 with 'Mesh download failed' instead of a bare 500 (matches the pattern already used in the /generate/text route L921-936).

Optional: document the preview-Cloudflare-eats-502-bodies behaviour in the AIGenerateDialog error-parsing code, or extract the JSON detail server-side into a non-502 status (e.g. 400 with detail) so users see the actionable 'Exhausted balance' text through the CDN.

The review-request expectation that image/multi-image would 502 due to exhausted balance is INCORRECT for the current implementation — fal_client.submit_async only queues (no balance check at submission time). Only text-to-3d's Flux Schnell step (subscribe_async) surfaces the exhausted-balance error at submission. This is a spec/impl mismatch worth clarifying in the iter-132 PRD.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py L810-844: _pick_ai_provider() + _provider_module() correctly encode the priority order (BYO Meshy → fal.ai → platform Meshy legacy) and default to Meshy for unknown/legacy provider strings. Clean.

server.py L887-946: text-to-3D error handling is comprehensive — FalHTTPError, HTTPStatusError, TimeoutException, RequestError, RuntimeError all lead to a quota refund (when !personal_key) + a well-shaped 502/504. Good pattern.
server.py L989-1002, L1048-1068: image and multi-image error handlers deliberately only refund the quota for platform (non-personal) users — verified correct.
server.py L1102: provider defaults to 'meshy' for legacy jobs — matches spec (backward compat). Verified by test_poll_legacy_no_provider_defaults_to_meshy.
server.py L1148-1151: mesh download only catches HTTPStatusError; bare 500 leak on RequestError. Minor — see action items.
fal_service.py L57-77: _api_key() + _ensure_configured() mirror env into os.environ[FAL_KEY] so fal_client picks it up even if tests stub the env late. Sound.

fal_service.py L142-167: multi-image only uses the first URL (Hunyuan3D v2 limitation) — documented in docstring. UI shouldn't advertise multi-view fusion for fal path unless PRD confirms this tradeoff is accepted.
fal_service.py L127-139: image endpoint accepts data:URL directly which fal.ai natively supports — nice, avoids upload-first roundtrip.
server.py /api/ai/usage L858-863: active_provider defaults to 'meshy' when neither BYO nor fal is configured. Consistent with the L822 comment about legacy fallback.

success_rate.backend: 100% — 14/14 new integration tests + 11/11 pre-existing fal_service unit tests pass

test_credentials: Uses mongosh-seeded sessions per /app/memory/test_credentials.md pattern. Test users prefixed TEST_ITER132_ and cleaned up in module teardown fixture.
seed_data_creation: Per-test users + sessions with TEST_ITER132_ prefix, plus mock ai_jobs docs for polling/download dispatch tests. All cleaned up in teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two critical gotchas: (1) The preview Cloudflare edge REPLACES all 502 response bodies with an HTML 'Bad gateway' page — content-type flips from application/json to text/html. Any test that needs to read JSON out of a 502 MUST hit http://localhost:8001 directly (INTERNAL_URL in the test file). Status-code assertions still work over the public URL. (2) fal.ai balance state is not the '502 always' scenario the review request assumed — text-to-3d may return 200 with a successful submission, image/multi-image always 200 (submit_async queues without balance check). The test file handles both cases. (3) Cleanup teardown lives in the `mongo` module-scoped fixture — anything you add with TEST_ITER132_ prefix gets swept.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bug fixes required. All routing/persistence/refund logic verified working. The one improvement suggestion (mesh-download RequestError catch) is a defensive hardening, not a functional break — dead-url mesh downloads are already blocked upstream by job.status != SUCCEEDED gate.
